australia.title: Australia beyond the visa checklist
australia.lead: Sunlight, long commutes in the big capitals, and strong coffee culture shape daily life. Use these planning tiles to picture cash flow, first-month admin, and how city pace differs from regional towns.
australia.disclaimer: Figures are illustrative planning ranges for migrants and students—not quotes, averages from a single source, or financial advice. Always verify rent, tax, and fees in the suburb you are considering.

costOfLiving.contextNote: Healthcare carries Medicare levy context once you are on eligible visas; private cover is common while settling.

dayInLife.title: Light before heat
dayInLife.body: Early walk or gym—summer mornings are the kindest window before the sun climbs.

dayInLife.title: Commute calculus
dayInLife.body: Train or bus in capitals; regional life often means a shorter drive and easier parking.

dayInLife.title: Lunch strip
dayInLife.body: Laneway cafes in Melbourne, harbour views in Sydney, food courts near campuses everywhere.

dayInLife.title: After work
dayInLife.body: Beach swim, river ride, or quiet suburb—distance to nature varies sharply by postcode.

dayInLife.title: Admin hour
dayInLife.body: Banks close early; many tasks move to apps. Keep PDFs of visa grants and IDs ready.

dayInLife.title: Quiet curfew
dayInLife.body: Noise rules differ by council; apartment blocks often have stricter evening norms than houses.

arrivalChecklist.title: First-month arrival rhythm
arrivalChecklist.subtitle: Order varies by visa; use this as a conversation starter with your planner, not a legal sequence.

steps.label: Gather passport and visa grant
steps.detail: You apply online through the ATO; processing is usually quick when details match immigration records.

steps.label: Keep TFN private
steps.detail: Only employers, banks, and your super fund need it—avoid sharing in casual chats or unsecured forms.

steps.label: Compare everyday vs migrant-friendly packs
steps.detail: Some banks offer limited-ID onboarding before you have local proof of address.

steps.label: Plan proof-of-address path
steps.detail: Lease letter, utility bill, or university correspondence—requirements differ by institution.

steps.label: Recognised licence window
steps.detail: States set how long you may drive on an overseas licence; after that, local tests apply.

steps.label: ID for rentals
steps.detail: Passport plus secondary ID speeds up real-estate portals and police checks.

steps.label: Prepaid SIM first
steps.detail: Airport kiosks work in a pinch; compare data caps before locking a contract.

steps.label: NBN vs 5G home
steps.detail: Rental properties vary—confirm what is included before buying hardware.

rows.city: Higher for comparable proximity to CBD and campuses.
rows.regional: Often lower; watch limited stock in boom towns.

rows.city: More verticals and meetups; competition is fiercer.
rows.regional: Smaller market; personal referrals carry more weight.

rows.city: Strong trains/trams; tolls and parking sting.
rows.regional: Car dependency rises; fuel and maintenance matter more.

rows.city: 24-hour options, dense cultural calendar.
rows.regional: Quieter evenings; some specialist services require travel.

new-zealand.title: New Zealand: wind, walks, and wet winters
new-zealand.lead: Life skews outdoors when weather allows. Housing stress exists in Auckland and Wellington; South Island towns trade salary bands for space.
new-zealand.disclaimer: Illustrative figures for orientation only. Verify with local listings and official fee schedules.

costOfLiving.contextNote: Earthquake and weather resilience show up in insurance and heating bills—ask locals about actual winter costs.

dayInLife.title: Coast or commute
dayInLife.body: Ferries in Wellington, motorways in Auckland—choose housing with realistic wind and rain tolerance.

dayInLife.title: Café density
dayInLife.body: Excellent flat whites; lunch lines move fast near CBD towers.

dayInLife.title: Trail time
dayInLife.body: Regional parks fill after work; always pack a layer—the temperature drops fast.

dayInLife.title: DIY culture
dayInLife.body: Trades can book out; learning basic maintenance saves stress in older villas.

dayInLife.title: Quiet evenings
dayInLife.body: Retail hours end earlier than many global cities—plan groceries accordingly.

dayInLife.title: Stargazing south
dayInLife.body: Clear nights in the South Island are spectacular; light pollution is low outside main towns.

arrivalChecklist.title: Settling in Aotearoa
arrivalChecklist.subtitle: Practical admin varies by visa category—confirm with official INZ guidance.

steps.label: Apply once you have an address path
steps.detail: Employers need your IRD for PAYE; banks may ask during account opening.

steps.label: Proof stack
steps.detail: Passport, visa, and proof of address—student halls often issue letters for this.

steps.label: Conversion rules
steps.detail: Some countries convert cleanly; others require theory or practical tests after a grace period.

steps.label: Eligible vs visitor
steps.detail: Understand what is covered before you skip travel insurance overlap windows.

rows.city: Tighter vacancy, higher rent for similar quality.
rows.regional: More space; some towns have limited rental choice.

rows.city: Higher nominal salaries in corporate roles.
rows.regional: Lower rents can offset smaller pay packets.

rows.city: Windy corridors, humidity in Auckland summers.
rows.regional: Cooler winters inland; heating costs can surprise.

rows.city: Diverse diaspora networks.
rows.regional: Smaller circles; integration can feel faster if you join local clubs.

canada.title: Canada: seasons define the budget
canada.lead: Winter gear, heating, and transit passes are non-negotiable line items. Cities feel North American in pace; suburbs stretch driving distances.
canada.disclaimer: Illustrative CAD amounts for planning conversations only. Provinces set health coverage and tax differently.

costOfLiving.contextNote: Add a winter buffer: clothing, higher utilities, and occasional rideshare spikes during storms.

dayInLife.title: Skyway or subway
dayInLife.body: PATH in Toronto, Métro in Montréal—learn underground routes before February.

dayInLife.title: Food halls
dayInLife.body: Multicultural lunch scenes are a highlight; tipping culture affects your cash flow.

dayInLife.title: Cold snap reality
dayInLife.body: Commute times stretch with snow; remote-friendly employers help sanity.

dayInLife.title: Community ice
dayInLife.body: Skating rinks and climbing gyms replace beach culture for many newcomers.

dayInLife.title: Quiet bylaws
dayInLife.body: Snow clearing and noise rules are strict—good neighbour habits matter.

dayInLife.title: Northern summer
dayInLife.body: Long daylight hours in June—energy bills flip later in the year.

arrivalChecklist.title: First Canadian milestones
arrivalChecklist.subtitle: Provinces issue health cards and driver licences—timelines differ.

steps.label: Service Canada
steps.detail: Book or queue with passport and permit that shows work/study conditions.

steps.label: Credit history gap
steps.detail: Secured cards help; keep rent receipts for future references.

steps.label: Exchange vs test
steps.detail: Some countries swap licences; others restart the graduated system.

steps.label: Waiting periods
steps.detail: Private insurance may be required during the gap—read the fine print.

rows.city: High in Toronto/Vancouver corridors.
rows.regional: Lower headline rent; heating and car costs may rise.

rows.city: Robust networks where funded.
rows.regional: Car almost essential; winter maintenance is real.

rows.city: English/French mix by region—Montréal rewards French effort.
rows.regional: English may dominate outside Québec, but check locally.

rows.city: Huge diaspora hubs.
rows.regional: Volunteer orgs become social lifelines faster.

united-kingdom.title: United Kingdom: compact cities, layered history
united-kingdom.lead: Walkability is high in many centres, but energy bills and council tax bite. Learn the postcode personality before you sign a lease.
united-kingdom.disclaimer: GBP figures are illustrative planning aids only. London is its own economy compared with the rest of the UK.

costOfLiving.contextNote: Council tax band sits outside this calculator—add it mentally when comparing flats.

dayInLife.title: Tube crush or canal jog
dayInLife.body: Peak Northern line vs leafy towpaths—your postcode sets the vibe.

dayInLife.title: Meal deal culture
dayInLife.body: Supermarkets compete on £4 lunch bundles; independents shine on weekends.

dayInLife.title: Pub as living room
dayInLife.body: Social life clusters locally; learn last-orders timing in your borough.

dayInLife.title: Paperwork evening
dayInLife.body: NHS app, bank switches, and council portals—keep PDFs organised.

dayInLife.title: Thin walls
dayInLife.body: Victorian conversions mean sound travels—headphones are essential.

dayInLife.title: Winter dark
dayInLife.body: Vitamin D and bright lamps help short daylight months.

arrivalChecklist.title: UK arrival anchors
arrivalChecklist.subtitle: Rules depend on visa route—use GOV.UK for authoritative sequencing.

steps.label: Share codes
steps.detail: Employers and landlords verify digitally—know how to generate and refresh them.

steps.label: Proof cascade
steps.detail: Council tax letter, utility bill, or university letter—each bank differs.

steps.label: Interview slots
steps.detail: Book early; payroll can start before the number arrives in some cases.

steps.label: Catchment areas
steps.detail: Register near home; some surgeries cap new patients.

rows.city: High rent; salaries bump but not always proportionally.
rows.regional: Lower rent; some sectors pay a London weighting remotely.

rows.city: Oyster + legs; ULEZ and congestion add costs if driving.
rows.regional: Buses dominate; intercity trains pricey without railcards.

rows.city: Infinite listings; sensory overload is real.
rows.regional: Strong local scenes; travel to London for big gigs.

rows.city: Grey stretches feel long between buildings.
rows.regional: Coast and peaks closer for weekend resets.

student-australia.title: Studying in Australia: cash, campus, and coast
student-australia.lead: Share houses, casual work caps, and semester intensity shape a different spreadsheet from skilled migrants. Picture a realistic week before you choose a city.
student-australia.disclaimer: Illustrative student-style budgets only. Course fees, OSHC, and Genuine Student requirements sit on official Department pages—this panel does not replace them.

costOfLiving.contextNote: Remember OSHC, textbooks, and society fees separately—they swing totals more than coffee.

dayInLife.title: Lecture sprint
dayInLife.body: Back-to-back tutorials—carry snacks; campus food queues spike between classes.

dayInLife.title: Library deep work
dayInLife.body: Air-con battles in summer; bookmark silent floors early.

dayInLife.title: Shift overlap
dayInLife.body: Retail and hospo shifts often start mid-afternoon—plan commute buffers.

dayInLife.title: Group assignment
dayInLife.body: Timezone pain for teammates overseas; shared docs save version chaos.

dayInLife.title: Cheap eats crawl
dayInLife.body: Suburb night markets vs CBD dining—learn the student specials map.

dayInLife.title: Quiet hours
dayInLife.body: Share-house noise rules keep friendships intact during exam weeks.

arrivalChecklist.title: Student arrival rhythm
arrivalChecklist.subtitle: Check your CoE, visa conditions, and institution orientation pack alongside this list.

steps.label: Match dates
steps.detail: Course start, visa grant, and flight landing should align with orientation expectations.

steps.label: Coverage window
steps.detail: Know what is excluded; keep digital cards on your phone.

steps.label: Campus pop-ups
steps.detail: Orientation week often hosts banks and telcos—compare before you commit.

steps.label: Hours caps
steps.detail: Track policy updates; payslips should reflect lawful hours.

rows.city: More listings; higher baseline rent.
rows.regional: Tighter market; car-sharing circles matter.

rows.city: More shifts, more competition.
rows.regional: Fewer employers; reliability becomes your brand.

rows.city: Clubs and diaspora societies abound.
rows.regional: Smaller cohorts; deeper friendships if you lean in.

rows.city: Weekend trips to beaches or ranges.
rows.regional: Nature on the doorstep; fewer late-night amenities.

global-pathways.title: Four destinations, four daily rhythms
global-pathways.lead: When you are torn between hubs, contrast lifestyle friction—not only points tables. Tap a country hub below for the full primer.
global-pathways.disclaimer: Qualitative snapshot for planning conversations. Numbers are broad bands, not rankings.

compareCards.name: Australia
compareCards.rentBand: High in Sydney/Melbourne; regional offsets possible
compareCards.climateVibe: Hot summers, mild winters in the north; south has cooler winters
compareCards.transport: Strong CBD trains; car common regionally
compareCards.jobFeel: Skills lists drive narratives; hospitality always hiring in cities

compareCards.name: Canada
compareCards.rentBand: Toronto/Vancouver premium; heating adds winter cost

compareCards.name: United Kingdom
compareCards.rentBand: London is an outlier; regional cities gentler
compareCards.climateVibe: Mild but grey; daylight swings sharply
compareCards.transport: Walk + rail; driving less central in London
compareCards.jobFeel: Sponsor licence roles concentrated in certain sectors

compareCards.name: New Zealand
compareCards.transport: Ferries and buses; car outside main corridors
compareCards.jobFeel: Smaller market; who you know matters sooner
